results.description: DESCRIPTION:FUTURE PANDEMICS WILL REQUIRE THAT WASTEWATER SURVEILLANCE FOR POTENTIALLY HARMFUL PATHOGENS SUCH AS SARS-COV-2 (THE VIRUS THAT CAUSES COVID-19) BE DEPLOYED QUICKLY AND EFFECTIVELY AND WITH RESULTS THAT CAN BE APPLIED LOCALLY. THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A HIERARCHICAL WASTEWATER SURVEILLANCE SENTINEL SITE IDENTIFICATION SYSTEM FOR DETECTION OF PATHOGENS THAT COULD CAUSE FUTURE PANDEMICS.ACTIVITIES:THE RESEARCH TEAM WILL IDENTIFY WASTEWATER SENTINEL SITES USING DATA-DRIVEN PREDICTIONS OF INFECTION RISK LEVELS AT VARIOUS SPATIAL RESOLUTIONS BASED ON AN INTEGRATED ANALYSIS OF DATA INCLUDING WEATHER, HUMAN MOBILITY, HEALTH CARE, INFRASTRUCTURE, POPULATION DENSITY, SOCIODEMOGRAPHICS, AND DATA FROM THE CURRENT COVID-19 PANDEMIC.SUBRECIPIENT:FUNDS ARE REQUESTED FOR A TOTAL OF $124,902 ($99,996 REQUESTED PLUS $24,906 COST SHARE) FOR A SUBAWARD TO THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA TO DEVELOP A RISK FORECASTING MAP. IN ADDITION, $124,681 ($99,631 REQUESTED PLUS $25,050 COST SHARE) IS REQUESTED FOR A SUBAWARD TO PORTLAND STATE UNIVERSITY TO ANALYZE TRAFFIC NETWORKS AND INCORPORATE THE DATA AND ANALYSIS INTO THE RISK FORECASTING MODEL. IN ADDITION, $70,000 IS BUDGETED FOR A SUBAWARD TO THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF COUNTY AND CITY HEALTH OFFICIALS (NACCHO), TO DEVELOP A STRATEGY TO VERIFY THE SAMPLING METHOD DEVELOPED BY THE GRANTEE AND TO CONTRIBUTE IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE COMPUTATIONAL PLATFORM.OUTCOMES:THE RECIPIENT WILL SUBMIT ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS ON THEIR EFFORTS TO DEVELOP A HIERARCHICAL WASTEWATER SENTINEL SITE IDENTIFICATION SYSTEM. THE EXPECTED OUTCOME IS THAT WASTEWATER SENTINEL SITES WOULD BE IDENTIFIED HIERARCHICALLY FROM THE COUNTY LEVEL, THE CITY/TOWN/VILLAGE LEVEL AND IDEALLY TO BUILDING LEVEL DEPENDING ON PATHOGEN TRANSMISSION CHARACTERISTICS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT WILL BE PUBLIC HEALTH DEPARTMENTS ACROSS THE U.S., AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
